description: Migration: `reports_to` column exists on `users` table and is nullable UUID
Migration: `reports_to` FK references `users(user_id)` — inserting invalid UUID fails
Migration seed: users who are team members get `reports_to` set to their team's `manager_id`
Migration seed: team managers do not get `reports_to` set to themselves
Migration seed: users in multiple teams (same manager) get correct `reports_to` value
Migration seed: users not in any team have `reports_to = NULL`
Cycle prevention: setting A.reports_to = B where B.reports_to = A is rejected
Cycle prevention: setting A.reports_to = A (self-reference) is rejected
Cycle prevention: deep chain cycle (A→B→C→D→A) is detected and rejected
Cycle prevention: valid chain (A→B→C, setting D→C) is accepted
`IUser` interface includes optional `reports_to` field
User edit form: 'Reports To' dropdown is visible when `teams-v2` flag is on
User edit form: 'Reports To' dropdown is hidden when `teams-v2` flag is off
User edit form: 'Reports To' dropdown lists all users in the tenant except the current user
User edit form: saving a valid `reports_to` value persists to database
User edit form: saving a `reports_to` value that creates a cycle shows error
User edit form: clearing `reports_to` (set to NULL) persists correctly
Org chart: displays tree with correct parent-child relationships based on `reports_to`
Org chart: users with `reports_to = NULL` appear as top-level root nodes
Org chart: hidden when `teams-v2` flag is off
`isInReportsToChain(B, A)` returns true when A.reports_to = B (direct report)
`isInReportsToChain(C, A)` returns true when A→B→C (transitive — two levels)
`isInReportsToChain(D, A)` returns true for deep chain A→B→C→D (three+ levels)
`isInReportsToChain(X, A)` returns false when X is not in A's reporting chain
`isInReportsToChain` handles arbitrarily deep chains without stack overflow
Timesheet approval: manager can approve direct report's timesheet via `reports_to` chain when flag is on
Timesheet approval: manager-of-manager can approve indirect report's timesheet via `reports_to` chain when flag is on
Timesheet approval: existing team-manager approval still works regardless of flag state
Timesheet approval: `reports_to` chain check is NOT active when flag is off
Timesheet approval: user with `timesheet:read_all` can still approve anyone (unchanged)
`fetchTimeSheetsForApproval` returns timesheets from `reports_to` subordinates when flag is on
AvailabilitySettings: shows `reports_to` subordinates in user list when flag is on
SchedulePage: includes `reports_to` subordinates in managed users scope when flag is on
Migration: `role` column exists on `team_members` with default value 'member'
Migration: existing team members matching `teams.manager_id` have `role = 'lead'`
Migration: `assigned_team_id` column exists on `tickets` table and is nullable
Migration: `assigned_team_id` column exists on `project_tasks` table and is nullable
`ITicket` interface includes optional `assigned_team_id`
`IProjectTask` interface includes optional `assigned_team_id`
Team assignment to ticket (no primary): sets `assigned_team_id`, sets lead as `assigned_to`, adds other members as `ticket_resources`
Team assignment to ticket (no primary): lead is NOT added as `ticket_resources` (would violate DB constraint)
Team assignment to ticket (existing primary): `assigned_to` stays unchanged, all team members added as resources except current `assigned_to`
Team assignment to ticket (existing primary is the lead): lead not double-assigned, other members added as resources
Team assignment: resources added via team have `role = 'team_member'` in `ticket_resources`
Team assignment: members already present as individual `ticket_resources` are not duplicated
Team assignment to project task: same logic works for `project_tasks`
`UserAndTeamPicker`: renders users section and teams section with visible separator
`UserAndTeamPicker`: typing in search filters both users and teams
`UserAndTeamPicker`: teams display member count and lead name
`UserAndTeamPicker`: teams use generic team icon, users use avatar
`UserAndTeamPicker`: selecting a team triggers team assignment action
`UserAndTeamPicker`: selecting a user works identically to existing `UserPicker`
Feature flag swap: `UserAndTeamPicker` renders on ticket detail when `teams-v2` is on
Feature flag swap: `UserAndTeamPicker` renders on task detail when `teams-v2` is on
Feature flag swap: `UserPicker` renders unchanged when `teams-v2` is off
Team badge: chip appears on 'Agent team' card when `assigned_team_id` is set and flag is on
Team badge: chip shows generic team icon + team name + x button
Team badge: chip follows same styling as Additional Agents chips
Team badge: hidden when `teams-v2` flag is off even if `assigned_team_id` has a value
Team removal: clicking x on team badge opens confirmation dialog
Team removal: 'Remove all' removes `ticket_resources` with `role = 'team_member'` and clears `assigned_team_id`
Team removal: 'Keep all' clears `assigned_team_id` only, all resources stay
Team removal: individual checkboxes allow selective member removal
Team removal: primary `assigned_to` is never automatically removed
Team removal: manually-added agents (not `role = 'team_member'`) are not affected by 'Remove all'
Independence: adding a new individual agent does not affect team badge or `assigned_team_id`
Independence: removing an individual agent does not affect team badge or `assigned_team_id`
Independence: SLA escalation adding an agent does not trigger team removal
Snapshot: changing team roster after assignment does not update existing ticket resources
'Assigned To' filter: shows teams in dropdown alongside users when flag is on
'Assigned To' filter: selecting a team filters tickets by `assigned_team_id`
'Assigned To' filter: selecting a user still filters by `assigned_to` (existing behavior preserved)
'Assigned To' filter: shows only users (no teams) when flag is off
Workflow: `tickets.create` with `assignee: { type: 'team', id: teamId }` assigns team correctly
Workflow: `tickets.create` with `assignee: { type: 'user', id: userId }` still works (existing behavior)
Tenant export includes `reports_to` column from users
Tenant export includes `assigned_team_id` from tickets and project_tasks
Tenant export includes `role` from team_members
Multi-tenant: `reports_to` queries are tenant-scoped
Multi-tenant: team assignment queries are tenant-scoped
Multi-tenant: `assigned_team_id` filter queries are tenant-scoped
